Global sentiment remained cautious due to rising oil prices and inflation concerns. Focus now shifts to upcoming second-quarter earnings from major U.S. technology firms. Analysts noted the gains reflected a technical rebound ahead of key corporate results.

business.title: Cause for concern? HDFC Bank is lagging ICICI Bank where it matters as investors count Rs 5 lakh crore loss
business.summary: HDFC Bank's stock has declined significantly this year, losing considerable investor value. Analysts note a widening operating gap between HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank. Margins are under pressure for HDFC Bank, while loan growth lags peers. ICICI Bank shows stronger earnings, better margins, and faster credit growth. Investors await margin recovery and management stability for HDFC Bank.

Revenue from operations increased 28% to Rs 2,448 crore, while total income rose 22% to Rs 2,630 crore. Profit before tax climbed to Rs 247 crore, reflecting stronger operating performance both year-on-year and quarter-on-quarter.

Authorities have intervened to cool speculative fever and curb leveraged trading. Analysts suggest foreign investor selloffs are moderating, with a neutral stance on the market.

Investors should focus on strength and discipline amid this market rotation.
